howitworks.title: How it works
howitworks.subtitle: From Korea to your driveway in ~45-60 days.
howitworks.step1Body: Choose from auctions, dealers, or private sellers.
howitworks.step2Body: Invoice, export certificate, and bill of lading prepared.
howitworks.step3Body: Korea to Uzbekistan shipping arranged and tracked.
howitworks.step4Body: Duty, VAT, and import formalities handled for you.
howitworks.step5Body: Local delivery, registration support, and handover.

track.note: 2 SHIPMENTS IN TRANSIT · 1 AT KOREAN PORT · LIVE GPS

items.question: How do I buy a car through AUTOAUCTION?
items.answer: Browse the catalogue, click Request consultation on a car you like, and our team gets back to you within 24 hours to confirm the price and start the export process.

items.question: Where do the cars come from?
items.answer: We source from three channels: Korean auctions, licensed dealer platforms, and verified private sellers in Korea. Every car is inspected before purchase.

items.question: How long does delivery take?
items.answer: Around 45–60 days from purchase to plates. Shipping itself is 30–45 days (Korea → China → Kazakhstan → Uzbekistan), plus customs clearance and registration in Uzbekistan.

items.question: What documents are needed to export the car?
items.answer: Car invoice, export certificate, Bill of Lading (issued after shipping), and a copy of the buyer's passport. Our forwarding partner prepares everything for you.

items.question: How is the car shipped?
items.answer: By Ro-Ro (Roll-on/Roll-off) or by container from a Korean port. Route: Korea → China → Kazakhstan → Uzbekistan via rail and truck.

items.question: What customs fees apply in Uzbekistan?
items.answer: Import duty, VAT, recycling fee, and customs processing. The total depends on engine size, manufacture year, and the car's price. A licensed broker handles the calculation and paperwork.

items.question: What's the difference between 'ready' and 'crashed' cars?
items.answer: Ready cars are inspected and ready to drive. Crashed cars are sold as-is with a full damage report and a list of required replacement parts so you know exactly what to expect.

items.question: How do I get my Uzbek license plates?
items.answer: After customs clearance you receive the car in your city. Register it at the traffic police (GAI) and they issue Uzbek plates — usually the same day.

items.question: Are the prices final?
items.answer: Yes. Listed prices are fixed — no bidding wars and no surprise markups. Customs fees and shipping are quoted separately and depend on the destination city and engine size.

items.question: Can I get a consultation before buying?
items.answer: Of course. Open any car and click Request consultation, or use the Get consultation button on the homepage. Our specialists in Seoul and Tashkent reply within 24 hours.
